title: BCG Moreau Vaccine Safety Profile and NK Cells-Double Protection Against Disseminated BCG Infection in Retrospective Study of BCG Vaccination in 52 Polish Children with Severe Combined Immunodeficiency.
ehr_evidence: hospitalized in Children’s Memorial Health Institute, Warsaw (CMHI), in the years 1980–2015
summary: A retrospective study evaluating the safety profile and reactogenicity of the BCG Moreau vaccine in Polish children with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID), comparing them to an international cohort.
key_findings: Significantly lower rate of disseminated BCG infections (19% vs 34% in the Marciano study).
No deaths in patients treated with anti-TB drugs except one with low NK cell counts.
Significantly higher number of hematopoietic stem cell transplantations (HSCT) in the CMHI study.
population: 52 Polish children with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID)
